title: Self-Introduction in English
description: Tell the world who you are!

scenes.speech: Self-intro is your strongest weapon! Three sentences and you're set ✨
scenes.title: Start with You
scenes.content: This lesson is about English self-introduction. Three short sentences for name, likes, and dream — and you can make friends anywhere.

scenes.speech: Start with the name! Say I'm ___!
scenes.title: Say Your Name
scenes.content: I'm Mika. My name is Mika. Either works. Slow down a bit so the other person catches it.

scenes.speech: Adding where you're from opens conversation!
scenes.title: Where You're From
scenes.content: I'm from Japan. Add your city for impact: I'm from Tokyo, Japan. is friendly and clear.

scenes.title: Age and Grade
scenes.content: I'm 10 years old. I'm a 5th grader. Learn the number+unit and the grade form.
scenes.highlight:
I'm 10 years old.
I'm a 5th grader.

scenes.speech: Sharing one favorite thing keeps the talk alive!
scenes.title: What You Like
scenes.content: I like soccer. I love reading. Love is stronger than like. Pick one specific thing so the listener can ask back.

scenes.speech: Sharing your dream lifts the impression!
scenes.title: Your Dream
scenes.content: I want to be a programmer. My dream is to make games. The 'want to be ___' form lands well.
scenes.highlight:
I want to be a ___ .
My dream is to ___ .

scenes.speech: Wrap up with 'Nice to meet you!'
scenes.title: Nice to meet you!
scenes.content: Nice to meet you! is the magic line for first meetings. Reply with 'Nice to meet you, too!' for the perfect close.
scenes.highlight: Nice to meet you! → Nice to meet you, too!

scenes.title: 3-Sentence Template
scenes.content: 1) name 2) what you love 3) dream — in order. Short and clean lands in the listener's memory.
scenes.highlight:
1. I'm ___ .
2. I love ___ .
3. I want to be ___ .

scenes.title: Speak It Out
scenes.content: In front of a mirror, with a smile, out loud — best practice. Recording and listening back makes your fixes obvious.

scenes.speech: Quiz! How do you say 'Nice to meet you' in English?
scenes.quizQuestion: Which is the standard greeting for a first meeting?
scenes.quizExplanation: Nice to meet you! is the classic. Reply with the same plus 'too'.
